visual_notes: Nephrite's waxy sheen and subtle translucency are distinctive. White mutton-fat jade has a soft luminosity unlike any other stone. The green varieties can look similar to jadeite but typically show a more subdued, less intense green. The fibrous microstructure is visible under magnification as a tangled felt-like mass.
prompt_fragments: waxy spinach-green nephrite with fibrous felt texture
ancient jade carving millennia of tradition
vibe_notes: Nephrite is the older jade — the stone that launched millennia of Chinese and Pacific jade culture before jadeite arrived. Its mutton-fat white and spinach green are among the most culturally loaded color-material combinations in human history. It is not the most beautiful stone, but it may be the most meaningful.
folklore_summary: In Chinese tradition, jade is said to have a 'living' quality — it absorbs the essence of its wearer over time and gives back healing energy. It is said to feel alive in a way that other stones do not. A jade bangle that breaks is said to have 'sacrificed itself' to protect its owner from harm.
